problem_statement: An astronomical observatory image indexer on newly provisioned hosts stops accepting detector channels several hours into a night. Existing channels and the health endpoint remain active, so dashboards show a growing stale subset while raw images stay on disk. Determine the initiating constraint and corrective target using host snapshots, service attestations, and isolated proof evidence.

observations.statement: The stale subset begins when the 1013th long-lived detector channel becomes active; channels already open continue indexing, while each later channel logs a resource-unavailable accept failure.
observations.provenance: channel registry and indexer event log

observations.statement: A read-only process snapshot at first failure shows 1024 open descriptors against a hard ceiling of 1024; 1009 descriptors correspond to active detector streams and the remainder to logs, sockets, and the health endpoint.
observations.provenance: captured process descriptor table and limits file

observations.statement: The service unit on new hosts inherits a 1024 descriptor ceiling, while the archived unit from prior hosts explicitly sets 8192; application settings request up to 1400 concurrent channels in both environments.
observations.provenance: service-manager property dump and signed deployment bundles

observations.statement: Application binary, component inventory, detector map, stream protocol, and incoming image files match a prior host that indexed all 1180 channels during the same observing plan.
Descriptor count rises one-for-one with active detector channels and falls one-for-one when a channel naturally closes; no monotonic growth remains after channel count is held constant.
observations.provenance: process snapshots correlated with channel lifecycle ledger

observations.statement: Disk capacity, inode usage, network loss, process memory, and CPU remain normal at the threshold; raw images for rejected channels continue accumulating with valid checksums.
The green health check reuses an already-open socket and queries only existing channels, explaining why it remains successful after new stream acceptance has stopped.
observations.provenance: health-check route trace and descriptor ownership snapshot

observations.statement: An isolated host with the same binary and 1400 synthetic long-lived streams accepts only 1009 under the new unit and all 1400 when the prior ceiling is restored; stream contents and settings are unchanged.

A post-change endurance proof at 1400 channels stays below the revised ceiling, indexes all retained sample images, and adds an alert on remaining descriptor headroom rather than total process liveness.
observations.provenance: isolated endurance proof and monitoring assertion report
